speaker: Pablo
text: Last session you watched Morley Finch rally close to a hundred Charau-ka in Bwamandu, learned he plans to march them to the West Gate in two nights, bankrolled Fister for drinks, and debated the Charisma plan versus recruiting the Lord of Slime from the undercity tunnels.
sourceText: Last session you watched Morley Finch rally close to a hundred Charau-ka in Bwamandu, learned he plans to march them to the West Gate in two nights, bankrolled Fister for drinks, and debated the Charisma plan versus recruiting the Lord of Slime from the undercity tunnels.

speaker: Lisa
text: We can do both. Fister keeps them drunk and listening while Andy works the crowd, and we ask the slime thing to gum up whatever the mining company is doing in the sewers.
sourceText: We can do both. Fister keeps them drunk and listening while Andy works the crowd, and we ask the slime thing to gum up whatever the mining company is doing in the sewers.

speaker: Dave
text: I like both. Morley is the showman; the mining company is the paperwork. Cut the paperwork and his speech gets easier.
sourceText: I like both. Morley is the showman; the mining company is the paperwork. Cut the paperwork and his speech gets easier.

speaker: Victoria
text: I will tell Aliciet we are doing both. She wanted eyes on Morley, not a bar fight tonight.
sourceText: I will tell Aliciet we are doing both. She wanted eyes on Morley, not a bar fight tonight.

speaker: Pablo
text: You settle accounts with Fister—ten gold upfront, another ten if the crowd stays through tomorrow night. He grins and starts pulling casks from the back.
sourceText: You settle accounts with Fister—ten gold upfront, another ten if the crowd stays through tomorrow night. He grins and starts pulling casks from the back.

speaker: Lisa
text: And no singing contests this time. Last time you turned it into a wrestling match.
sourceText: And no singing contests this time. Last time you turned it into a wrestling match.

speaker: Pablo
text: That was art, lass. Art.
sourceText: That was art, lass. Art.

speaker: Dave
text: While Kadira handles Fister, Neo, do you want me to draft the speech tonight or wait until we know what the mining company paperwork says?
sourceText: While Kadira handles Fister, Neo, do you want me to draft the speech tonight or wait until we know what the mining company paperwork says?

speaker: Victoria
text: Draft tonight. Morley already poisoned the well with adventure stories. I want something that sounds like home, not like a hunter's trophy room.
sourceText: Draft tonight. Morley already poisoned the well with adventure stories. I want something that sounds like home, not like a hunter's trophy room.

speaker: Pablo
text: Through the magnet coin link Aliciet answers before you leave the bar: she heard your whisper about West Gate. She wants you at her office at first light with anything you learn about the Brass District meeting.
sourceText: Through the magnet coin link Aliciet answers before you leave the bar: she heard your whisper about West Gate. She wants you at her office at first light with anything you learn about the Brass District meeting.

speaker: Lisa
text: First light is in like four hours. Can we nap?
sourceText: First light is in like four hours. Can we nap?

speaker: Pablo
text: You can try. Bwamandu does not nap quietly.
sourceText: You can try. Bwamandu does not nap quietly.

speaker: Pablo
text: Morning in the Brass District. Aliciet's office is already busy—messengers, a ledger open to East Mwangi Mining Company shipments. She pours tea that tastes like expensive punishment.
sourceText: Morning in the Brass District. Aliciet's office is already busy—messengers, a ledger open to East Mwangi Mining Company shipments. She pours tea that tastes like expensive punishment.

speaker: Pablo
text: Morley Finch met a company factor last night near the old counting house. My watchers lost him in the fog, but they heard the phrase "bulk indenture." That is not hunting vocabulary.
sourceText: Morley Finch met a company factor last night near the old counting house. My watchers lost him in the fog, but they heard the phrase "bulk indenture." That is not hunting vocabulary.

speaker: Victoria
text: We think he is packaging Charau-ka volunteers as laborers. Fister is keeping the volunteers drunk and Andy is working on a counter-speech.
sourceText: We think he is packaging Charau-ka volunteers as laborers. Fister is keeping the volunteers drunk and Andy is working on a counter-speech.

speaker: Dave
text: And we are also visiting our friend in the undercity—the Lord of Slime—to see if it can clog company drains before anything moves.
sourceText: And we are also visiting our friend in the undercity—the Lord of Slime—to see if it can clog company drains before anything moves.

speaker: Pablo
text: Do not say "Lord of Slime" in my office. Say "sanitation consultant."
sourceText: Do not say "Lord of Slime" in my office. Say "sanitation consultant."

speaker: Lisa
text: Sanitation consultant. Got it. Do you still want us tailing Morley, or hitting the mining company first?
sourceText: Sanitation consultant. Got it. Do you still want us tailing Morley, or hitting the mining company first?

speaker: Pablo
text: Both, if you can survive it. I will seed rumors of slavers at the North Gate the way you asked. Keep Morley in Bwamandu if you can—Golanthus has closed the White Marks tree hotels to outsiders, so Morley's "famous tree dwellings" bait will not work there.
sourceText: Both, if you can survive it. I will seed rumors of slavers at the North Gate the way you asked. Keep Morley in Bwamandu if you can—Golanthus has closed the White Marks tree hotels to outsiders, so Morley's "famous tree dwellings" bait will not work there.

speaker: Pablo
text: She slides four brass tokens across the desk—passes for her "eyes" network. Each bears a tiny magnet mark.
sourceText: She slides four brass tokens across the desk—passes for her "eyes" network. Each bears a tiny magnet mark.

speaker: Dave
text: These worth anything if we get arrested?
sourceText: These worth anything if we get arrested?

speaker: Pablo
text: They are worth more if you do not get arrested. Next question.
sourceText: They are worth more if you do not get arrested. Next question.

speaker: Victoria
text: Darkwing—did your people see anything? Kadira keeps mentioning it like it is a person.
sourceText: Darkwing—did your people see anything? Kadira keeps mentioning it like it is a person.

speaker: Pablo
text: Darkwing is a ghost story sailors tell when cargo goes missing. Last week three Aspis Consortium crates turned up empty near the ferry slip. My clerk wrote "Darkwing" as a joke. I do not joke about empty crates.
sourceText: Darkwing is a ghost story sailors tell when cargo goes missing. Last week three Aspis Consortium crates turned up empty near the ferry slip. My clerk wrote "Darkwing" as a joke. I do not joke about empty crates.

speaker: Lisa
text: So maybe not Jekyll. Maybe just thieves.
sourceText: So maybe not Jekyll. Maybe just thieves.

speaker: Pablo
text: Aliciet taps the mining ledger. Roll Society if you want to know how East Mwangi Mining Company usually moves "indentured" workers.
sourceText: Aliciet taps the mining ledger. Roll Society if you want to know how East Mwangi Mining Company usually moves "indentured" workers.

speaker: Victoria
text: Twenty-two. They use river barges at night, not the West Gate crowd march. Morley is theater; the company is the exit.
sourceText: Twenty-two. They use river barges at night, not the West Gate crowd march. Morley is theater; the company is the exit.

speaker: Dave
text: Eighteen. They had a similar contract up in Bloodcove. "Volunteer resettlement." Same ink, different city.
sourceText: Eighteen. They had a similar contract up in Bloodcove. "Volunteer resettlement." Same ink, different city.

speaker: Pablo
text: Aliciet nods. That matches her fear: Morley fills the stage; the company handles the ledger.
sourceText: Aliciet nods. That matches her fear: Morley fills the stage; the company handles the ledger.

speaker: Pablo
text: You descend through the south wall sewer tunnel Fister pointed out—slime, rat bones, and a smell like wet copper. The undercity opens into a chamber where bioluminescent fungi paint the ceiling green.
sourceText: You descend through the south wall sewer tunnel Fister pointed out—slime, rat bones, and a smell like wet copper. The undercity opens into a chamber where bioluminescent fungi paint the ceiling green.

speaker: Lisa
text: Lord of Slime? It is us. We brought offerings.
sourceText: Lord of Slime? It is us. We brought offerings.

speaker: Pablo
text: Something shifts in the muck—a detritus eater the size of a pony, all pseudopods and polite interest. It rumbles like a kettle.
sourceText: Something shifts in the muck—a detritus eater the size of a pony, all pseudopods and polite interest. It rumbles like a kettle.

speaker: Pablo
text: Offerings. Offerings. You are late. The ferryman said you would come yesterday.
sourceText: Offerings. Offerings. You are late. The ferryman said you would come yesterday.

speaker: Dave
text: We were dealing with a famous hunter and a hundred monkeys. You still want mining company sludge?
sourceText: We were dealing with a famous hunter and a hundred monkeys. You still want mining company sludge?

speaker: Pablo
text: Sludge yes. Also names. Names taste better on contracts.
sourceText: Sludge yes. Also names. Names taste better on contracts.

speaker: Victoria
text: East Mwangi Mining Company. Factor named Henslow, if the ledger is right. Can you block their outflow pipe near the Brass District?
sourceText: East Mwangi Mining Company. Factor named Henslow, if the ledger is right. Can you block their outflow pipe near the Brass District?

speaker: Pablo
text: It extends a pseudopod like a handshake. Roll Diplomacy or Nature—your call.
sourceText: It extends a pseudopod like a handshake. Roll Diplomacy or Nature—your call.

speaker: Victoria
text: Nineteen. We are not asking you to eat people. Just paperwork and pumps.
sourceText: Nineteen. We are not asking you to eat people. Just paperwork and pumps.

speaker: Pablo
text: Paperwork crunchy. Pumps crunchy. I go tonight. You bring more offerings tomorrow or I eat the ferryman.
sourceText: Paperwork crunchy. Pumps crunchy. I go tonight. You bring more offerings tomorrow or I eat the ferryman.

speaker: Lisa
text: We will bring garbage from Fister's kitchen. You will love it.
sourceText: We will bring garbage from Fister's kitchen. You will love it.

speaker: Pablo
text: It subsides into the tunnel toward the Brass District. You mark the pipe on Kadira's map with a slime smear.
sourceText: It subsides into the tunnel toward the Brass District. You mark the pipe on Kadira's map with a slime smear.

speaker: Pablo
text: Afternoon at Fister's. The crowd is thicker—maybe eighty Charau-ka, some from last night, some new faces drawn by free ale. Morley is not here; Remy was seen skulking near the marketplace instead.
sourceText: Afternoon at Fister's. The crowd is thicker—maybe eighty Charau-ka, some from last night, some new faces drawn by free ale. Morley is not here; Remy was seen skulking near the marketplace instead.

speaker: Pablo
text: Your money is working. They are drinking the good stuff and not asking who pays. When do you want the speech?
sourceText: Your money is working. They are drinking the good stuff and not asking who pays. When do you want the speech?

speaker: Dave
text: Sunset. When the light hits the wall and everyone feels poetic and stupid.
sourceText: Sunset. When the light hits the wall and everyone feels poetic and stupid.

speaker: Victoria
text: I will stand where Morley stood. If he returns, I want him to have to talk over me.
sourceText: I will stand where Morley stood. If he returns, I want him to have to talk over me.

speaker: Lisa
text: I will watch the alleys. Last time a Corsair solved an argument with a rifle shot.
sourceText: I will watch the alleys. Last time a Corsair solved an argument with a rifle shot.

speaker: Pablo
text: Mr. Andy climbs onto an upturned crate. Fister kills the drums. The crowd hushes—not clean silence, but the kind that happens before a storm.
sourceText: Mr. Andy climbs onto an upturned crate. Fister kills the drums. The crowd hushes—not clean silence, but the kind that happens before a storm.

speaker: Dave
text: You have heard stories about gates and gold. I am here to tell you about roots. Roots do not travel in chains. Roots drink where they are planted.
sourceText: You have heard stories about gates and gold. I am here to tell you about roots. Roots do not travel in chains. Roots drink where they are planted.

speaker: Pablo
text: He speaks for ten minutes—American Gods cadence, local details, names of Bwamandu alleys instead of highways. Roll Performance.
sourceText: He speaks for ten minutes—American Gods cadence, local details, names of Bwamandu alleys instead of highways. Roll Performance.

speaker: Pablo
text: Twenty-seven. A good chunk of the crowd cheers. A dozen Charau-ka look unsettled in the way people look when a story hits a scar. Three leave quietly toward the marketplace.
sourceText: Twenty-seven. A good chunk of the crowd cheers. A dozen Charau-ka look unsettled in the way people look when a story hits a scar. Three leave quietly toward the marketplace.

speaker: Victoria
text: I follow the three who left—see if they are going to Remy.
sourceText: I follow the three who left—see if they are going to Remy.

speaker: Pablo
text: Neo slips out. Kadira watches the roofline. Mr. Andy accepts a tankard from Fister and does not drink it.
sourceText: Neo slips out. Kadira watches the roofline. Mr. Andy accepts a tankard from Fister and does not drink it.

speaker: Pablo
text: Neo tracks the three Charau-ka to a brass merchant's alley where Remy trades copper for paper. A human in company livery—Henslow, if Aliciet's ledger is right—waits with a wax seal kit.
sourceText: Neo tracks the three Charau-ka to a brass merchant's alley where Remy trades copper for paper. A human in company livery—Henslow, if Aliciet's ledger is right—waits with a wax seal kit.

speaker: Victoria
text: I whisper through the coin link: company factor, three signatures, Brass District back alley.
sourceText: I whisper through the coin link: company factor, three signatures, Brass District back alley.

speaker: Lisa
text: Copy. Andy, keep the crowd. Dave and I will not make it in time—Neo, can you get a photograph of the seal?
sourceText: Copy. Andy, keep the crowd. Dave and I will not make it in time—Neo, can you get a photograph of the seal?

speaker: Victoria
text: It is Pathfinder. I do not have a photograph.
sourceText: It is Pathfinder. I do not have a photograph.

speaker: Pablo
text: Roll Stealth to hold position without being spotted by Remy.
sourceText: Roll Stealth to hold position without being spotted by Remy.

speaker: Pablo
text: Twenty-one. Neo watches Henslow press three Charau-ka thumb marks into a contract headed "Volunteer Transit to Brownbrook." Remy pockets coin and hisses at them to return to Fister's crowd so nobody notices.
sourceText: Twenty-one. Neo watches Henslow press three Charau-ka thumb marks into a contract headed "Volunteer Transit to Brownbrook." Remy pockets coin and hisses at them to return to Fister's crowd so nobody notices.

speaker: Victoria
text: They are signing people before the West Gate march. Morley is the parade; this is the checkout line.
sourceText: They are signing people before the West Gate march. Morley is the parade; this is the checkout line.

speaker: Pablo
text: Henslow leaves toward the counting house. Neo copies the contract header onto a scrap—good enough for Aliciet.
sourceText: Henslow leaves toward the counting house. Neo copies the contract header onto a scrap—good enough for Aliciet.

speaker: Pablo
text: Back at Fister's, Morley Finch arrives at dusk with two Corsairs and applause like he owns the district. He launches into a hunting story before he notices Mr. Andy still on the crate.
sourceText: Back at Fister's, Morley Finch arrives at dusk with two Corsairs and applause like he owns the district. He launches into a hunting story before he notices Mr. Andy still on the crate.

speaker: Pablo
text: Well! An encore. Do you sing, friend, or do you only preach?
sourceText: Well! An encore. Do you sing, friend, or do you only preach?

speaker: Dave
text: I preach. You hunt. Tonight the crowd can choose which story feeds them.
sourceText: I preach. You hunt. Tonight the crowd can choose which story feeds them.

speaker: Pablo
text: Roll Diplomacy or Intimidation—group aid from Fister's crowd if you invoke Bwamandu.
sourceText: Roll Diplomacy or Intimidation—group aid from Fister's crowd if you invoke Bwamandu.

speaker: Pablo
text: The crowd splits— cheers for both men. Morley smiles too wide. Remy slips away again.
sourceText: The crowd splits— cheers for both men. Morley smiles too wide. Remy slips away again.

speaker: Lisa
text: Neo, did you get the paper?
sourceText: Neo, did you get the paper?

speaker: Victoria
text: Volunteer Transit to Brownbrook. Three signatures already. Henslow is moving them before the gate speech.
sourceText: Volunteer Transit to Brownbrook. Three signatures already. Henslow is moving them before the gate speech.

speaker: Dave
text: Brownbrook is where the main Corsair band camped. This is not city slavery—it is export.
sourceText: Brownbrook is where the main Corsair band camped. This is not city slavery—it is export.

speaker: Pablo
text: Through the magnet link Aliciet answers: block the march if you can, but do not lose the contract scrap. She is sending a clerk to meet you at the sewer grate in one hour.
sourceText: Through the magnet link Aliciet answers: block the march if you can, but do not lose the contract scrap. She is sending a clerk to meet you at the sewer grate in one hour.

speaker: Lisa
text: Lord of Slime should have hit the pumps by now. Want to check if the Brass District is flooding?
sourceText: Lord of Slime should have hit the pumps by now. Want to check if the Brass District is flooding?

speaker: Pablo
text: You detour toward the counting house. Greenish water burbles from a grate—slime clog working. Company guards shout inside about ruined manifests.
sourceText: You detour toward the counting house. Greenish water burbles from a grate—slime clog working. Company guards shout inside about ruined manifests.

speaker: Pablo
text: Henslow storms out, coat ruined, screaming for someone named Darkwing as if it were a person who could fix pipes.
sourceText: Henslow storms out, coat ruined, screaming for someone named Darkwing as if it were a person who could fix pipes.

speaker: Victoria
text: Darkwing again. I am starting to think Aliciet's clerk was not joking.
sourceText: Darkwing again. I am starting to think Aliciet's clerk was not joking.

speaker: Lisa
text: Or Henslow is just blaming the ghost when his sludge monster misfires.
sourceText: Or Henslow is just blaming the ghost when his sludge monster misfires.

speaker: Pablo
text: A shadow crosses the roof—too fast for a person, too quiet for a bird. Neo catches a glimpse of something with a wide cloak and no face. It is gone before she can point.
sourceText: A shadow crosses the roof—too fast for a person, too quiet for a bird. Neo catches a glimpse of something with a wide cloak and no face. It is gone before she can point.

speaker: Victoria
text: Did anyone else see that?
sourceText: Did anyone else see that?

speaker: Dave
text: No. And I do not want to.
sourceText: No. And I do not want to.

speaker: Pablo
text: At the sewer grate Aliciet's clerk—a tired woman named Sesi—takes Neo's scrap, verifies the wax pattern, and swears quietly in three languages.
sourceText: At the sewer grate Aliciet's clerk—a tired woman named Sesi—takes Neo's scrap, verifies the wax pattern, and swears quietly in three languages.

speaker: Pablo
text: This is enough for Dr. Cordova to freeze company assets inside the city. You need one more thing: a named Corsair officer on the contract.
sourceText: This is enough for Dr. Cordova to freeze company assets inside the city. You need one more thing: a named Corsair officer on the contract.

speaker: Lisa
text: Captain Togaloo? Morley mentioned the band north of the wall.
sourceText: Captain Togaloo? Morley mentioned the band north of the wall.

speaker: Pablo
text: Togaloo or Finch. Preferably both. Bring proof before tomorrow night or the West Gate march becomes legal on paper even if it is rotten in fact.
sourceText: Togaloo or Finch. Preferably both. Bring proof before tomorrow night or the West Gate march becomes legal on paper even if it is rotten in fact.

speaker: Pablo
text: You return to Fister's to find the crowd doubled—Morley's show drawing listeners, Fister's ale keeping them soft. Golanthus's White Marks guards watch from a distance but do not enter Bwamandu.
sourceText: You return to Fister's to find the crowd doubled—Morley's show drawing listeners, Fister's ale keeping them soft. Golanthus's White Marks guards watch from a distance but do not enter Bwamandu.

speaker: Pablo
text: Tomorrow at sunset, West Gate! Who wants to see the world beyond Kibwe?
sourceText: Tomorrow at sunset, West Gate! Who wants to see the world beyond Kibwe?

speaker: Dave
text: Who wants to see Bwamandu still standing when you return?
sourceText: Who wants to see Bwamandu still standing when you return?

speaker: Pablo
text: Another Performance opposed check—Morley is good at this.
sourceText: Another Performance opposed check—Morley is good at this.

speaker: Lisa
text: He wins the moment, not the crowd. Look—half of them are cheering both of you. That is fine.
sourceText: He wins the moment, not the crowd. Look—half of them are cheering both of you. That is fine.

speaker: Victoria
text: We need Morley alone long enough to lift a signed order. Pickpocket, bribery, or asking nicely?
sourceText: We need Morley alone long enough to lift a signed order. Pickpocket, bribery, or asking nicely?

speaker: Dave
text: Bribery looks like his language. He betrays parties for trophy heads. Offer him a bigger trophy.
sourceText: Bribery looks like his language. He betrays parties for trophy heads. Offer him a bigger trophy.

speaker: Lisa
text: The Gorilla King story again? He already sold that ship.
sourceText: The Gorilla King story again? He already sold that ship.

speaker: Dave
text: Not the King. Darkwing. Henslow blamed it on the roof. Morley is a hunter—he will want the credit.
sourceText: Not the King. Darkwing. Henslow blamed it on the roof. Morley is a hunter—he will want the credit.

speaker: Victoria
text: We do not know what Darkwing is.
sourceText: We do not know what Darkwing is.

speaker: Dave
text: Exactly. Perfect bait.
sourceText: Exactly. Perfect bait.

speaker: Pablo
text: You spend an hour crafting the lie: Darkwing roosts in the old Aspis warehouse, steals manifests, hates loud crowds. Kadira will sell it to Morley at the bar.
sourceText: You spend an hour crafting the lie: Darkwing roosts in the old Aspis warehouse, steals manifests, hates loud crowds. Kadira will sell it to Morley at the bar.

speaker: Lisa
text: Morley. Friend. Heard you like impossible things.
sourceText: Morley. Friend. Heard you like impossible things.

speaker: Pablo
text: Impossible things pay well when stuffed correctly. What have you got?
sourceText: Impossible things pay well when stuffed correctly. What have you got?

speaker: Lisa
text: Darkwing. Roof runner near Aspis crates. Your company friends are blaming it for their ruined ledgers tonight.
sourceText: Darkwing. Roof runner near Aspis crates. Your company friends are blaming it for their ruined ledgers tonight.

speaker: Pablo
text: Roll Deception.
sourceText: Roll Deception.

speaker: Pablo
text: Darkwing. I have heard whispers. If you know where it roosts, girl, you may have bought yourself five minutes of my attention.
sourceText: Darkwing. I have heard whispers. If you know where it roosts, girl, you may have bought yourself five minutes of my attention.

speaker: Lisa
text: Aspis warehouse after midnight. Bring quiet boots. And maybe leave Remy behind—he signs people away while you speechify.
sourceText: Aspis warehouse after midnight. Bring quiet boots. And maybe leave Remy behind—he signs people away while you speechify.

speaker: Pablo
text: Morley laughs—a hunter's laugh. He claps Remy on the shoulder hard enough to stagger him and says tomorrow is for gates, tonight is for trophies.
sourceText: Morley laughs—a hunter's laugh. He claps Remy on the shoulder hard enough to stagger him and says tomorrow is for gates, tonight is for trophies.

speaker: Victoria
text: He took the bait. If Darkwing is real, we are walking into something worse than Morley.
sourceText: He took the bait. If Darkwing is real, we are walking into something worse than Morley.

speaker: Dave
text: If Darkwing is fake, we isolate him from the crowd. Either way we win time.
sourceText: If Darkwing is fake, we isolate him from the crowd. Either way we win time.

speaker: Pablo
text: Midnight near the Aspis warehouse. Morley arrives with one Corsair, rifles ready, Goodheart swagger on full display. You lay ambush from the loading dock.
sourceText: Midnight near the Aspis warehouse. Morley arrives with one Corsair, rifles ready, Goodheart swagger on full display. You lay ambush from the loading dock.

speaker: Pablo
text: Show me this Darkwing. I have stuffed bigger lies than birds.
sourceText: Show me this Darkwing. I have stuffed bigger lies than birds.

speaker: Pablo
text: Something moves on the roof—cloak, no face, the same shape Neo saw above Henslow. Morley fires. The bullet passes through cloth and hits brick.
sourceText: Something moves on the roof—cloak, no face, the same shape Neo saw above Henslow. Morley fires. The bullet passes through cloth and hits brick.

speaker: Pablo
text: Darkwing drops into the alley—not flying, falling in slow pieces like torn shadow. It whispers a name none of you speak aloud. Morley goes pale.
sourceText: Darkwing drops into the alley—not flying, falling in slow pieces like torn shadow. It whispers a name none of you speak aloud. Morley goes pale.

speaker: Victoria
text: Not fake. Not a bird. What did it say?
sourceText: Not fake. Not a bird. What did it say?

speaker: Pablo
text: It said Togaloo owes a debt. That is not my hunt.
sourceText: It said Togaloo owes a debt. That is not my hunt.

speaker: Lisa
text: Good. Give us Captain Togaloo's marching orders and you can chase shadows all week.
sourceText: Good. Give us Captain Togaloo's marching orders and you can chase shadows all week.

speaker: Pablo
text: Morley hesitates—roll Intimidate or Diplomacy as a group.
sourceText: Morley hesitates—roll Intimidate or Diplomacy as a group.

speaker: Pablo
text: Fine. Togaloo signed transit papers yesterday. I signed spectacle. You want the paper, you keep me alive when Darkwing returns.
sourceText: Fine. Togaloo signed transit papers yesterday. I signed spectacle. You want the paper, you keep me alive when Darkwing returns.

speaker: Pablo
text: He pulls a folded order from his coat—Black Heart Corsairs letterhead, Captain Togaloo's signature, Volunteer Transit authorized. Sesi will weep when she sees it.
sourceText: He pulls a folded order from his coat—Black Heart Corsairs letterhead, Captain Togaloo's signature, Volunteer Transit authorized. Sesi will weep when she sees it.

speaker: Dave
text: Now we stop the West Gate march with law instead of bar tabs.
sourceText: Now we stop the West Gate march with law instead of bar tabs.

speaker: Pablo
text: Darkwing reforms on the roof and screams—silent, but you feel it in your teeth. Morley runs. The Corsair fires wild. Alarms start in the Brass District.
sourceText: Darkwing reforms on the roof and screams—silent, but you feel it in your teeth. Morley runs. The Corsair fires wild. Alarms start in the Brass District.

speaker: Victoria
text: We have the signature. We do not have the crowd.
sourceText: We have the signature. We do not have the crowd.

speaker: Lisa
text: Fister still has them drinking. Tomorrow is the hard part.
sourceText: Fister still has them drinking. Tomorrow is the hard part.

speaker: Pablo
text: You end the session sprinting through Bwamandu alleys—contract in hand, Darkwing loose, Morley Finch shouting for backup, and sunset tomorrow still coming for the West Gate.
sourceText: You end the session sprinting through Bwamandu alleys—contract in hand, Darkwing loose, Morley Finch shouting for backup, and sunset tomorrow still coming for the West Gate.
